The biggest highlight is the jump in adoption.
Adoption has grown across all departments.
The health score is higher than last quarter.
The customer said that the walkthrough had saved time.
If they get more support, their adoption will catch up.
Thanks for joining, David. I would like to start with the results: this quarter, adoption has grown from sixty to eighty percent across your teams.
That is a great number. My director will be happy to hear it. How did the support team do on the critical tickets?
Every critical ticket was resolved within the deadline. There is one honest concern, though: the finance teams are still slow to adopt.
I appreciate the honesty. What do you recommend we do about it?
I recommend a short extra training for finance. If they get more support, their adoption will catch up quickly. Then we review it in the next QBR.
That works for me. The partnership has clearly delivered value, so let us talk about renewing the contract early.

Hi Patricia, this is David from Northwind, just a quick note before our quarterly business review tomorrow. First, thank you, because this has been a strong quarter for my teams. I have seen the adoption numbers, and honestly they are better than I expected. On the call, I would like you to focus on three things. First, walk us through the highlights, especially how the support team handled those critical tickets, because my director will ask about that. Then, I want your honest view on the finance teams; their usage is still low, and I need a clear plan to fix it before I take this to the board. After that, we should talk about next year. If the value is there, I am open to renewing the contract early and adding more licenses, but I will need a strong business case to justify the cost to my boss. So please come with your recommendations and your numbers ready. See you tomorrow, and thanks again for the partnership.

Hi Patricia, it is Karen, your manager. I had a look at your slides for the Northwind QBR, and they are in great shape, so well done. I just want to share a few tips before you present. First, lead with the results, not the agenda; open with your strongest highlight, the jump in adoption, so the room is on your side from minute one. Then, when you reach the finance concern, do not hide it, but always pair the problem with a recommendation; say what you have noticed and what you suggest in the same breath. For the renewal, remember to talk about value before you talk about price; once the customer sees the return on investment, the contract conversation becomes much easier. And if David pushes back on the cost, stay calm and bring it back to the business case. You know this account better than anyone, so trust yourself. Go and show them what a real partnership looks like. Good luck, I know you will be great.
